heroHeadline: UK Inflation Jumps to 3.3% on Iran War Fuel Surge; FOMC 7 Days Away
heroSubtitle: Apr 22: UK CPI accelerated to 3.3%, driven by soaring fuel prices as the Iran war's oil supply disruption feeds through to consumer prices — adding stagflation pressure on the Bank of England alongside the Fed and ECB. WTI crude holding ~$95.75/bbl (Brent $96.32) after fully reversing the April 17 crash. The CAPE tariff refund portal ($166B IEEPA refunds) launched April 20 but crashed under high volume on Day 1. Kevin Warsh's Senate Banking confirmation hearing as Fed Chair nominee (replacing Powell, term expires May 15) concluded April 21. Moody's Analytics: 49% US recession probability. FOMC April 29-30 stagflation decision and ECB April 30 rate decision now 7 days away.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, Al Jazeera, Semafor
